body.title: Scraps: inference outcome learner (PA2.0 observe→discover loop)
body.result: built devices/scraps/jobs/inference_outcome_learner.py: reads MINION_RESULT+GRANNY_DISPATCH in one query, aggregates per (tag,task_class,size), writes outcome_report.json, posts OUTCOME_LEARNER_REPORT; 15 tests pass; outcome_summary() wired to context-load step 5.85
body.description:
MINION_RESULT channel posts contain rich outcome signal (signal, task_class, iterations, cost_usd, tokens_in, tokens_out, round_count post-D2) but nothing aggregates this into routing intelligence. This is the PA2.0 Layer 1→2 gap: observations exist, relationship discovery doesn't. Same signal also exists in Igor (per user) and should come OUT of Igor into a shared Scraps job.

Build a Scraps job (periodic run, not daemon) that:
1. Reads MINION_RESULT posts from shared channel (last N=200 or since ~/.granny/outcome_learner_cursor.txt)
2. Aggregates per tag, task_class (tier), and size: DONE%, avg_iterations, avg_cost_usd, ESCALATE%, REPROMPT%, UPGRADE%, BLOCKED%, CONFUSED%
3. Persists report to ~/.granny/outcome_report.json (flat file, overwrite on each run)
4. Posts OUTCOME_LEARNER_REPORT to shared channel with top 3 actionable routing insights (e.g. 'Platform/M/worker: 80% escalate — try analyst tier')
5. Exposes outcome_summary() for context-load hook

**Affected files:** new devices/scraps/jobs/inference_outcome_learner.py, new tests/scraps/test_outcome_learner.py; context-load skill (hook to call outcome_summary at step 5.8)
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; flat JSON output; no-hardcoded-instance-refs; log at INFO; log every interface crossing (channel read, file write)
**Scope boundary:** Read, aggregate, and report only. Out of scope: automatically updating rules_engine.py routing from patterns (that is a future PA2.0 Layer 3 ticket); extracting from Igor's internal metrics (out of scope — Igor metrics migration is a separate ticket if needed).
**Completion criteria:** `.venv/bin/pytest tests/scraps/ -q -k outcome_learner` passes with ≥3 tests (aggregate correctness, empty-channel graceful, OUTCOME_LEARNER_REPORT format); running against real channel data produces outcome_report.json with per-tag DONE% values; outcome_summary() callable from context-load
